prompt: The transpose method produces the wrong result for non-square matrices. What is the bug?
choices: The loop bounds i and j are swapped
new int[m.length][m.length] allocates a square matrix; for an r×c input the transposed result should be c×r, i.e., new int[m[0].length][m.length]
t[j][i] should be t[i][j] to correctly transpose
2D array transposition requires in-place swapping only
explanation: Transposing an r×c matrix produces a c×r matrix. Allocating new int[m.length][m.length] (r×r) is only correct when r==c. For non-square matrices it either wastes space or causes ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. Fix: new int[m[0].length][m.length].
